{{Short description="The Planck constant divided by 2π; fundamental constant of quantum mechanics}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Reduced Planck constant
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Quantum mechanics]], [[Planck constant]], [[Uncertainty principle]], [[Schrödinger equation]]
| symbol = ħ (h-bar)
| value = 1.054571817×10⁻³⁴ J·s
| definition = <math>\hbar = h/2\pi</math>
}}

The '''reduced Planck constant''' (or '''Dirac constant'''), denoted <math>\hbar</math> (h-bar), is the [[Planck constant]] <math>h</math> divided by <math>2\pi</math>:

:<math>\hbar = \frac{h}{2\pi} = 1.054571817 \times 10^{-34}\ \text{J·s}</math>

== Why <math>\hbar</math> appears naturally ==
The factor of <math>2\pi</math> appears because quantum phenomena are fundamentally associated with angular quantities (rotations, oscillations, waves). <math>\hbar</math> is the natural unit of [[angular momentum]] and appears throughout quantum mechanics.

== Key equations using <math>\hbar</math> ==

=== Schrödinger equation ===
:<math>i\hbar\frac{\partial}{\partial t}\Psi = \hat{H}\Psi</math>

=== Heisenberg uncertainty principle ===
:<math>\Delta x \, \Delta p \geq \frac{\hbar}{2}</math>

=== Commutation relation ===
:<math>[\hat{x}, \hat{p}] = i\hbar</math>

=== Planck–Einstein relation ===
:<math>E = \hbar\omega</math>

=== De Broglie wavelength ===
:<math>\lambda = \frac{2\pi\hbar}{p} = \frac{h}{p}</math>
:<math>p = \hbar k</math> (momentum–wavenumber relation)

=== Angular momentum quantization ===
:<math>L_z = m_\ell\hbar</math>

== Spin ===
[[Spin (physics)|Spin]] is measured in units of <math>\hbar</math>. The electron has spin <math>\hbar/2</math>. The spin operator eigenvalues are <math>S_z = m_s\hbar</math> with <math>m_s = \pm 1/2</math>.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Angular momentum]]
* [[Planck constant]]
* [[Quantum mechanics]]
* [[Spin (physics)]]
* [[Uncertainty principle]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Physical constants]]
[[Category:Quantum mechanics]]
